hypotheses.initial_data: u0 in H^s(), s > 5/2, div u0 = 0
hypotheses.continuation_condition: Integrability of the L-infinity norm of vorticity: integral_0^T ||(t)||_{L-inf} dt < inf

conclusion: The local strong solution u(t) extends for all t and remains smooth (Global Regularity) under the BKM condition.
lemmas_used: Logarithmic inequality for grad-u in L-inf (Kozono-Taniuchi, 2000)
Scaling invariance of the L3 and BMO-1 norms
points_non_demonstrated: Uniform global bound on ||(t)||_{L-inf} for arbitrary large initial data (The Millennium Problem)
Closing the gap between the Morrey-type local condition (C) and the critical Besov/Lorentz requirements without additional decay assumptions
Optimal constants for the log-inequality in Besov space endpoints

complexite_numerique_tests.time_stepping: Semi-implicit (AB2 for non-linear, Crank-Nicolson for diffusion)
complexite_numerique_tests.complexity: O(N^3 log N) per time step
complexite_numerique_tests.recommended_N: 64 to 256 for standard desktops; 512+ for high-fidelity intermittency study.
